summary: Backend verification of 3 pre-deploy security fixes (strong admin password, restricted CORS, scheduled price-check limit). Created /app/backend/tests/test_security_predeploy.py (15 tests, all PASS). Also re-ran admin RBAC regression suite with the new password (15/15 PASS). Old 'admin123' correctly rejected (401). Admin login with new strong password works and /auth/me returns role=admin. All core authenticated endpoints (products CRUD, pc-specs, pc-health, advisor stream, profiles, admin stats/users) verified functional and RBAC enforced (401 unauth, 403 non-admin).

minor.issue: Preview edge overrides backend CORS with 'access-control-allow-origin: *' AND 'allow-credentials: true' — browser-invalid combination and defeats the backend restriction *at the preview URL only*. Backend code (get_cors_origins) is correct as verified against localhost:8001 (rejects evil origins with 400 on preflight, echoes only ALLOWED_ORIGIN). This is an infra artifact of the preview environment, not a code bug; production deployment (which does not sit behind CF preview) will use the backend CORSMiddleware.

action_items: OPTIONAL: If preview URL is used by a real browser for the frontend during pre-prod QA, the CF-level '*' + credentials combo means fetch()/XHR credentialed requests will be rejected by the browser. Confirm whether the frontend runs under this preview host or a different origin before shipping.

OPTIONAL: seed_admin correctly updates existing admin's password when it changes (verified by successful new-password login and rejection of the old one), but LOCKOUT_MINUTES (15) counter on login_attempts is not cleared on successful admin seed rotation; not a blocker but worth noting.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py seed_admin: good — no weak fallback, rotates password if env differs from stored hash.
server.py scheduled_price_check: limit(100) with .sort('updated_at', 1) confirmed present at line ~45; PRICE_CHECK_BATCH constant used (clean).
settings.get_cors_origins strips '*' entries and always includes FRONTEND_URL; correct and minimal.

auth cookies set with secure=False in set_auth_cookies — acceptable for preview HTTP local, but in production over HTTPS this should be secure=True (currently HTTPS traffic still gets non-Secure cookies). Consider making secure driven by env before deploy.
verify_password swallows all exceptions returning False — fine; but ensures no timing side channel is present since bcrypt.checkpw is constant-time.
seed_data_creation: Several disposable users created via /auth/register (sec_*, reg_*, rbac_*) and cleaned up where possible.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The three pre-deploy security blockers are verified fixed at the backend code level. If future testing agents run CORS tests against the external preview URL, expect access-control-allow-origin: '*' due to Cloudflare edge; test CORS against http://localhost:8001 instead (as done in test_security_predeploy.py::TestCORS). Also note: old-password test intentionally exercises the failed-login lockout counter — running the security suite repeatedly (>5x in 15 min from same IP) can lock out the admin account for 15 min; use a distinct email for stress runs.
